title: Instrumentation plan for new checkout
description: A new checkout flow launches next month with zero analytics. Build the instrumentation plan so you can measure what matters from day one.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at ShelfPilot, a Series A retail tech company (84 employees, $5.9M ARR). Your immediate assignment is instrumentation plan for new checkout. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Optimize for repeatable outcomes over one-off wins that do not scale.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 95724
New logo adds: 1528
Activation rate: 48%
30-day retention: 33%
NPS: 30 (n=238)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 23%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: -2 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 8 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Asha Morris): "Buyers are asking for proof points, not intent. We need visible traction fast."
VP Engineering (Anya Gupta): "Unresolved platform dependencies make estimates look precise but wrong."
Head of Customer Success (Kira Park): "Escalations spike after handoff because expectations are set too optimistically."
Commercial note: 7 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 41 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Helio Health Group (Operations Lead): "We see value quickly once configured, but the first session still feels too heavy."
Orbit Travel Co (IT Manager): "A few avoidable errors in onboarding made the team question readiness."
Harbor Wholesale (Director of Product): "The concept is right, but edge-case handling still feels unfinished."
Data gap: support transcript tagging is inconsistent across segments.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 38
Median cycle time: 6 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 64%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 5
Release rollback events last quarter: 0
Average time to resolve escalations: 60 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Name one north star metric and explain why it captures durable user value.
submissionFields.label: North Star Metric

submissionFields.hint: Define supporting indicators and expected directional movement.
submissionFields.label: Leading and Lagging Metrics

submissionFields.hint: Interpret the current data while separating signal from noise.
submissionFields.label: Current Readout

submissionFields.hint: Identify blind spots, sample bias, or conflicting definitions.
submissionFields.label: Data Gaps and Biases

submissionFields.hint: Specify events, cohorts, and dashboards needed next.
submissionFields.label: Instrumentation and Reporting

rubric.name: Metric Quality
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Do metrics reflect user value over vanity output?

rubric.name: Analysis Rigor
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does interpretation account for bias, confounding, and sample limits?

rubric.name: Actionability
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are recommendations directly tied to observed signals?

rubric.name: Uncertainty Management
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are unknowns identified with a plan to resolve them?

expertSolution.content: Recommendation: use weekly active accounts completing the target workflow twice without support intervention as the north star. This ties value to repeat behavior across the 95724-account base rather than one-time activity.

Leading indicators: time to first value, setup completion, and error-free sessions. Lagging indicators: 30-day retention (33% baseline), expansion rate (23%), and escalations per 1,000 active accounts.

Evidence is directionally useful but not conclusive. Activation is 48% and retention is 33%, but NPS (30, n=238) is sample-biased and support pressure (41 escalations) suggests unresolved quality variance.

Key gaps are missing churned-account interviews, inconsistent transcript tagging, and uneven cohort coverage after week two. With 5 unresolved dependencies, instrumentation completeness must be treated as a delivery risk.

Instrument cohort events for activation steps, error states, and support touches. Publish weekly segmented dashboards with denominator checks and confidence notes so stakeholder claims are compared on consistent evidence.
